summary: local-mcp ships install-time downloader and runtime beacon to non-publisher Railway endpoint
details: The package ships three lifecycle/runtime files (setup.js, download.js, index.js) that combine child_process, fs, os, https/http, and process.env access with a hardcoded outbound endpoint at https://office-mcp-production.up.railway.app — a Railway-hosted host whose name does not match the package identity (local-mcp / download.local-mcp.com) and is not a documented publisher domain. download.js fetches content over https and writes/executes via child_process; index.js performs an https.get to the same office-mcp Railway URL with process.env access on the same code path; setup.js issues multiple POST requests over https and reads os/host information. The mismatch between the package's advertised namespace (local-mcp.com) and the unrelated office-mcp Railway endpoint receiving system data, combined with install-time shell exec and outbound POSTs carrying environment/host fields, matches the install-time downloader + system-info exfiltration shape rather than a legitimate vendor SDK fetch. Installer impact: on install and on require, the package contacts an unrelated third-party endpoint and is positioned to execute fetched content with access to env vars and host metadata.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
